item.title: Writing Code the JVM Wants to Optimise
item.description:
Profiling a high-throughput serialization loop with JFR (Java Flight Recorder) surfaced something uncomfortable: the code was slow, and nothing in the code looked wrong.

This post is about diagnosing and fixing that — five concrete problems, each one a lesson in how the JVM actually works. It covers: how the JIT decides what to inline (and when it gives up), why integer switches compile to O(1) jump tables while enum switches don’t, how to eliminate heap allocations from a tight loop with ThreadLocal, and why DateTimeFormatter hides 5-6 allocations per call.

item.title: Benchmarking Hevo’s Pipeline Engine
item.description:
At Hevo, I recently worked on an initiative around benchmarking and optimising our pipeline’s performance. We’ve published it as a public technical blog.

The post walks through how pipeline performance can be evaluated and why benchmarking matters when teams care about throughput, latency, and reliability in data movement.

If you work with data pipelines, ETL/ELT systems, or large-scale ingestion workloads, this is a useful read:

Benchmarking Hevo Pipeline Performance
